1.0.title: INTEGRATE CAREER AND TECHNICAL STUDENT ORGANIZATIONS (CTSOs)

1.1.title: Explore the History and Organization of CTSOs

1.1.1.description: Discuss the requirements of CTSO participation/involvement as described in Carl D. Perkins Law

1.1.3.description: Investigate the impact of federal and state government regarding the progression and operation of CTSOs (e.g., Federal Statutes and Regulations, Nevada Administrative Code [NAC], Nevada Revised Statutes [NRS])

1.2.1.description: Discuss the purpose of parliamentary procedure

1.2.2.description: Demonstrate the proper use of parliamentary procedure

1.2.3.description: Differentiate between an office and a committee

1.2.4.description: Discuss the importance of participation in local, regional, state, and national conferences, events, and competitions

1.2.5.description: Participate in local, regional, state, or national conferences, events, or competitions

1.2.6.description: Describe the importance of a constitution and bylaws to the operation of a CTSO chapter

1.3.1.description: Explore opportunities in community service-related work-based learning (WBL)

1.3.2.description: Participate in a service learning (program related) and/or community service project or activity

1.3.3.description: Engage with business and industry partners for community service

1.4.1.description: Demonstrate college and career readiness (e.g., applications, resumes, interview skills, presentation skills)

1.4.2.description: Describe the appropriate professional/workplace attire and its importance

1.4.3.description: Investigate industry-standard credentials/certifications available within this Career Cluster™

1.4.4.description: Participate in authentic contextualized instructional activities

1.4.5.description: Demonstrate technical skills in various student organization activities/events

1.5.title: Understand the Relevance of Career and Technical Education (CTE)

1.5.1.description: Make a connection between program standards to career pathway(s)

1.5.2.description: Explain the importance of participation and completion of a program of study

1.5.3.description: Promote community awareness of local student organizations associated with CTE programs

2.1.1.description: Create prototypes that use algorithms to solve computational problems by leveraging prior student knowledge and personal interests

2.1.2.description: Describe how artificial intelligence drives many software and physical systems

2.1.3.description: Implement an artificial intelligence algorithm (e.g., machine learning) to play a game against a human opponent or solve a problem

2.1.4.description: Use and adapt classic algorithms to solve computational problems

2.1.5.description: Develop classic algorithms in code to solve computational problems

2.1.6.description: Evaluate algorithms in terms of their efficiency, correctness, and clarity

2.2.1.description: Justify the selection of specific control structures when tradeoffs involve implementation, readability, and program performance, and explain the benefits and drawbacks of choices made

2.2.2.description: Design and iteratively develop computational artifacts for practical intent, personal expression, or to address a societal issue by using events to initiate instructions

2.2.3.description: Illustrate the flow of execution of a recursive algorithm

2.3.1.description: Demonstrate the use of both LinkedLists and ArrayLists to simplify solutions, generalizing computational problems instead of repeatedly using simple variables

2.3.2.description: Compare and contrast fundamental data structures and their uses

2.3.4.description: Implement ArrayLists and LinkedLists in code

2.4.1.description: Decompose problems into smaller components through systematic analysis using constructs such as procedures, modules, and/or objects

2.4.2.description: Create artifacts by using procedures within a program, combinations of data and procedures, or independent but interrelated programs

2.4.3.description: Construct solutions to problems using student-created components such as procedures, modules, and/or objects

2.4.4.description: Analyze a large-scale computational problem and identify generalizable patterns that can be applied to a solution

2.4.5.description: Demonstrate code reuse by creating programming solutions using libraries and APIs

2.5.1.description: Systematically design and develop programs for broad audiences by incorporating feedback from users

2.5.2.description: Evaluate software licenses that limit or restrict the use of computational artifacts when using resources such as libraries

2.5.3.description: Evaluate and refine computational artifacts to make them more usable by all and accessible to people with disabilities

2.5.4.description: Design and develop computational artifacts while working in team roles and using collaborative tools

2.5.5.description: Document design decisions using text, graphics, presentations, and/or demonstrations in the development of complex programs

2.5.6.description: Plan and develop programs for broad audiences using a software life cycle process (e.g., requirements, design, coding, testing, and deployment)

2.5.7.description: Explain security issues that might lead to compromised computer programs (e.g., public vs. private, encryption, buffer overflows, cybersecurity)

2.5.8.description: Develop programs for multiple computing platforms

2.5.9.description: Use version control systems, integrated development environments (IDEs), and collaborative tools and practices (code documentation) in a group software project

2.5.10.description: Develop and use a series of test cases to verify that a program performs according to its design specifications

2.5.11.description: Modify an existing program to add additional functionality and discuss intended and unintended implications (e.g., breaking other functionality)

3.1.1.description: Explain how abstractions hide the underlying implementation details of computing systems embedded in everyday objects

3.2.1.description: Compare levels of abstraction and interactions between application software, system software, and hardware layers

3.2.2.description: Categorize the roles of operating system software

3.2.3.description: Discuss the use of computer virtualization

3.3.1.description: Develop guidelines that convey systematic troubleshooting strategies that others can use to identify and fix errors

3.3.2.description: Illustrate ways computing systems implement logic, input, and output through hardware components

4.1.1.description: Translate between different bit representations of real-world phenomena, such as characters, numbers, and images (e.g., convert hexadecimal colors to decimal percentages, ASCII/Unicode representation)

4.1.2.description: Demonstrate the ability to store bit representation of real-world phenomena, characters, numbers, and images

4.2.title: Create Using Collection, Visualization, and Transformation

4.2.1.description: Create interactive data visualizations or alternative representations using software tools to help others better understand real-world phenomena

4.2.2.description: Use data analysis tools and techniques to identify patterns in data representing complex systems

4.2.3.description: Select data collection tools and techniques to generate data sets that support a claim or communicate information

5.1.title: Evaluate the Impact of Computing on Culture

5.1.1.description: Evaluate the ways computing impacts personal, ethical, social, economic, and cultural practices

5.1.2.description: Test and refine computational artifacts to reduce bias and equity deficits

5.1.3.description: Demonstrate ways a given algorithm applies to problems across disciplines

5.1.4.description: Explain the potential impacts of artificial intelligence on society

5.1.5.description: Evaluate computational artifacts to maximize their beneficial effects and minimize harmful effects on society

5.1.6.description: Discuss how computational innovations that have revolutionized aspects of our culture might evolve

5.2.1.description: Use tools and methods for collaboration on a project to increase connectivity of people in different cultures and career fields

5.2.2.description: Use tools and methods for collaboration to increase the productivity of a team

5.3.title: Explain Safety, Law, and Ethics Related to Computing

5.3.1.description: Explain the beneficial and harmful effects that intellectual property laws can have on innovation

5.3.2.description: Explain the privacy concerns related to the collection and generation of data through automated processes that may not be evident to users

5.3.3.description: Evaluate the social and economic implications of privacy in the context of safety, law, or ethics

5.3.4.description: Discuss the role of ethics in technologies

5.3.5.description: Discuss laws and regulations that impact the development and use of software

6.1.1.description: Evaluate the scalability and reliability of networks by describing the relationship between routers, switches, servers, topology, and addressing

6.1.2.description: Describe the issues that impact network functionality (e.g., bandwidth, load, delay, topology)

6.1.3.description: Discuss the difference between deploying software to the cloud versus the standard model (i.e., running software locally on a PC)

6.2.1.description: Illustrate how sensitive data can be affected by malware and other attacks

6.2.2.description: Recommend security measures to address various scenarios based on factors such as efficiency, feasibility, and ethical impacts

6.2.3.description: Compare various security measures, considering tradeoffs between the usability and security of a computing system

6.2.4.description: Explain tradeoffs when selecting and implementing cybersecurity recommendations

6.2.5.description: Compare ways software developers protect devices and information from unauthorized access (e.g., password protection, 2 factor authentication)

6.2.6.description: Utilize industry tools and best practices (e.g., code scanning tools, automated security scans during the development process) to create secure code that prevents vulnerabilities

7.1.title: Explain Workforce and Society Needs Related to New and

7.1.1.description: Describe job skills needed for potential careers in new and emerging technologies

7.1.2.description: Explore potential uses for and industries that may use emerging technologies

7.1.3.description: Explain the role of ethics as it relates to security and emerging technologies
